response_text:
:::writing
To: All Employees  
From: Leadership Team  
Subject: New Remote Work Policy (Effective Next Month)

Over the past several years, our team has successfully adapted to a variety of working arrangements. As we continue to grow, we want to create a clear, consistent policy that supports productivity, collaboration, and flexibility. Beginning next month, we will be implementing a new remote work policy for the company.

Policy Overview  
Our company will adopt a “Remote-First with In‑Person Collaboration” model.

• Employees may work remotely up to four days per week.  
• Each team will designate one common in‑office collaboration day per week.  
• Employees who prefer to work in the office more often are welcome to do so.  
• Fully remote arrangements may be approved for roles or individuals whose responsibilities do not require in‑person presence.

Managers will coordinate with their teams to determine the most effective in‑office day based on project needs, cross‑team collaboration, and customer commitments.

Why We’re Making This Change  
As a 120‑person software company, we are at a size where coordination and communication become increasingly important. While remote work has offered flexibility and improved focus for many people, we have also heard feedback about the value of regular in‑person collaboration for brainstorming, onboarding, mentoring, and complex problem solving.

This policy is designed to balance both priorities:  
• Preserve the flexibility many employees value.  
• Create predictable in‑person time for stronger collaboration.  
• Maintain a consistent structure across teams as we continue to grow.

What You Need To Do Next  
Over the next two weeks:

1. Managers will meet with their teams to select a weekly in‑office day.  
2. Employees with unique circumstances or interest in full‑time remote status should discuss this with their manager and HR.  
3. Please review the detailed policy document that HR will distribute later this week, which will include guidelines for scheduling, office space use, and remote work expectations.

The new policy will take effect on the first Monday of next month.

We appreciate the flexibility and professionalism everyone has shown as our work environment continues to evolve. Our goal is to support both great work and a sustainable work experience for the entire team.

If you have questions, please reach out to your manager or the HR team.

Thank you,  
Leadership Team
